w:r.w:t: “You log honestly. AI maps the gap between who you are and who you said you want to be.”
Self-logging • Free AI (Gemini 2.0 Flash) • iOS-friendly • 4–6 week MVP
Built first for Ahmad — to stop wasting his time and reach his independent dream.

The first NAFS document promised a lot of things that are technically impossible on iPhone — reading WhatsApp time from the OS, blocking apps with full-screen overlays, real-time activity surveillance. None of that is allowed by Apple to third-party apps.

So we redefined NAFS. The new product is honest, buildable on a free stack, and arguably stronger than v1 because it builds the muscle of self-reporting — which is more Islamic, more durable, and works on every phone.
where you log your day honestly, and a free AI model maps every entry to your stated dream — telling you, in days and percentages,
how close or far you are from becoming the person you said you want to be.
The Islamic foundation — Deen first, Jannah as the ultimate goal.
The Dream-to-Daily mapping idea — but now powered by a real math engine, not vibes.
Weekly AI report card on Sundays.
Replaced with honest self-logging + photo proof + commitment streaks.
No app blocking. Instead: smart notifications, the AI Tribunal, and the Mapping Engine showing real consequences.
Gemini 2.0 Flash (free tier) replaces paid Claude API. Total runtime cost ≈ $0.
11 modules → 6 in MVP. Ship in 4–6 weeks instead of 13.
Built as a mobile web app (PWA) first — works on iPhone Safari, no App Store, no $99 fee. Native iOS app later if it's worth it.

Smart Muslim students know what they want. Jannah. A successful career. Independence. Strong family. Discipline. They write down goals, watch YouTube videos about productivity, and start the week motivated.
Then their day gets eaten by WhatsApp, scrolling, distractions, and shame. By Friday they feel guilty. By Sunday they reset. The cycle repeats every week for years.
The core issue is not lack of apps to track time. The core issue is
the gap between what we say we want and what we actually do — and the fact that no one shows us the gap in numbers we can't lie about.
NAFS is a daily self-reporting app with one job: make the gap visible.
Every day, you log honestly. You write down what you did, attach a photo of your work as proof, and rate your prayers, study hours, dream-work hours, and time wasted.
The AI does the math. It pulls your stated dream, your past 30/60/90 days of logs, and computes:
— how many days closer or further from your dream today moved you.
— at your current pace, when will you actually arrive?
— what % of the person you said you want to be are you actually being?
— every Sunday, the AI Tribunal writes you a report you can't argue with.
How it actually works in v2
Islamic values are the floor, not a feature
Daily prayer log, Quran tracker, Hijri calendar, Jannah as the apex of every dream tree
Every action mapped to a numerical distance from your dream
Mapping Engine computes Today's Pull and Trajectory Forecast from real logs
Self-reporting is the practice — not a workaround
Photo proof, sadqa stakes, public dream board, weekly Tribunal — fake logging hurts you
Arabic for soul, self, the essence of a person — the thing the Quran tells us to master
Deep teal (#0F4C5C) + gold accent (#C9A227) on dark navy background
Premium, calm, slightly intimidating — like a wise mentor's office, not a kids' app
Mobile web app (PWA) — works on iPhone, Android, desktop. Add to Home Screen on iPhone.
Native iOS app via Expo (only if PWA proves the concept and budget allows)
Muslim students 16–30 who already have stated dreams and are tired of failing themselves
English in MVP. Urdu support in v1.1.
Supabase free tier (500 MB, unlimited rows, auth)
Google Gemini 2.0 Flash — free tier, 1,500 requests/day, 1M tokens/day
No $99 Apple Developer fee, no Mac required.
Same app works on Ahmad's iPhone 11, on his laptop, and on any Android user later.
on iPhone makes it look and feel like a native app — full-screen, app icon, push notifications via web push.
Push changes instantly without App Store review.

Web push notifications on iPhone require iOS 16.4+ and the app must be added to Home Screen first. Your iPhone 11 supports this if it's on iOS 16.4 or higher (released March 2023). If you're on older iOS, you'll need to update before notifications work.
3. The Mapping Engine — How NAFS Actually Computes Your Dream Distance
This is the heart of NAFS. Every other feature serves this engine.
During onboarding, the user defines their dream in three layers:
One sentence. e.g., “Become an independent software engineer earning $3,000/month by December 2027.”
With AI's help, the app estimates the total focused hours needed. (e.g., 3,000 hours of deep coding + project work).
Shipping a project / writing production code
This is what the dream actually requires
3.2 The Math — How Today's Pull Is Computed
After every daily log, the engine computes:
# Required pace (computed once at dream setup)
"Today pulled you 4.0 days closer. Yesterday pulled you 0.6 days further."
Every Sunday, the AI looks at your last 30 days of weighted hours and projects forward:
verdict = f"At your current pace you arrive {arrival_date.strftime('%B %Y')} — {delay_days:.0f} days late."
verdict = "You are ahead of your stated pace. Don't slow down."
This is the line that hits hardest. Computed daily as a weighted average across four dimensions:
Prayers logged on time, Quran read today, dhikr completed
Sleep on time, screen time within limit, streak intact
Exercise logged, water, meals not skipped
The output is shown as a single sentence on the home screen:
the person you said you want to be.”
4.1 MVP (the 6 modules that ship in 4–6 weeks)
Identity Score, Today's Pull, Trajectory line, prayer countdown, dream image, quick log button
Dream definition, activity weights, Trajectory Forecast, public dream board (always visible)
End-of-day form: prayers, dream-work hours, study, sleep, screen time, mood. Photo proof. Voice notes.
5 prayers tap-log, Quran pages, dhikr counter, prayer heatmap, Hijri date
Custom challenges with sadqa stakes, streak counter, break-streak donation flow
Today's Pull explanation, Sunday Tribunal report, Future Self Letter delivery, Ask AI chat
University module (assignments, GPA, attendance) — Ahmad already has this in his head.
Skills tracker with radar chart — adds complexity, not core to dream-mapping.
Knowledge module (books, courses) — folds into Dream Work logging in MVP.
Communication tracker — too vague to log honestly.
Health module — basic sleep/screen-time goes in Daily Log, full version later.
Friend witness / social features — only after solo version proves itself.
4.3 The Five Amazing Features (your picks, expanded)
After every daily log, the home screen flashes a single line:
Every Sunday, NAFS projects your current 30-day pace forward and shows you a chart of two lines:
The dream pace line — what you said you'd do.
The actual pace line — what you're really doing.
Where they meet (or don't) is your projected arrival date.
Live percentage on the home screen. Computed by the formula in section 3.4. It is the most uncomfortable, most useful number in the app.
Feature D — Weekly AI Tribunal (Sunday 8 PM)
Every Sunday at 8 PM, Gemini generates a brutally honest report. Not generic. Not encouraging without data. It reads YOUR data and writes a verdict.
Sample output below in section 6 (AI Prompts).
During onboarding (and any time after), the user writes a letter to their future self — to who they will be in 1 year, 3 years, or at the dream date.
Once a month, NAFS surfaces one letter at a random time. The user reads it. The AI then writes a one-paragraph response from their CURRENT data:
“Dear future me, by 2027 I want to be earning my own money so my parents don't worry about me anymore.”
AI reply from your current data:

“Ahmad, your future self wrote this 7 months ago. Since then you've averaged 2.1 hours/day on dream work — your own pace requires 9.0. At this rate, your future self will be writing the same letter in May 2027. You still have time. The next 24 hours can change the line on the chart.”

Every Daily Log can attach a photo. The screen of your code, the page of your book, the empty plate after iftar, the prayer rug. The photo isn't shared anywhere — it's only for you to see in your past logs. But the act of taking the photo forces honesty.
Bonus: Gemini Vision (also free tier) can optionally describe what's in the photo — so logs become searchable later. (“Show me every day I worked on ChronoVault.”)
When creating a challenge, the user optionally sets a sadqa amount per broken day (e.g., Rs. 100 per missed day).
If the user breaks the streak, NAFS:
Calculates the total owed (e.g., missed 3 days × Rs. 100 = Rs. 300).
Provides one-tap links to verified Pakistani charities (Edhi, Saylani, Al-Khidmat) — NAFS does not collect the money.
Asks for a screenshot of the donation receipt within 48 hours, otherwise the streak doesn't reset.
This converts failure into a Deen win. You either keep the streak, or you turn breaking it into sadqa.
The dream is not buried in a settings menu. It is the wallpaper of the home screen — a photo, a sentence, and the Trajectory line — visible every time the app opens. You cannot avoid your own dream.
In v1.1, an option to make the dream board visible on the iPhone Lock Screen widget will be added.
Strong enough for pattern recognition, summarization, coaching tone
$0.075 / 1M input tokens, $0.30 / 1M output tokens — extremely cheap if exceeded
Get free API key from aistudio.google.com — 2 minutes
5.2 Daily AI Budget for One User
Estimated calls per active user per day:
1× Monthly Future Self reply (once per month)
Total: ~5–10 requests/day per user. Free tier handles ~150–300 active users for free.
5.3 The 8 AI Roles in NAFS v2
After every log, writes one direct sentence: how today moved you and what tomorrow needs.
Brutally honest weekly report. References your numbers. Never generic.
Scans last 30 days, flags the largest gap between stated dream and actual behavior.
Finds correlations: 'Every Friday your score drops. Sleep on Thursday < 6 hrs predicts it.'
Reads your old letters and writes a present-day reply grounded in current data.
Conversational interface to your own data: 'why am I not improving?'
Reads photo proof; tags it; makes logs searchable in the future.
Breaks the weekly report into modular sections so it never feels copy-pasted.
These are the actual prompts to use in the Gemini API. Each one assumes user data is injected at runtime.
You are NAFS — a strict but caring AI accountability coach for a Muslim
student. The user just logged their day. You have their numbers.
Line 1: One sentence stating Today's Pull in days (e.g., "+2.3 days" or "−1.6 days") with a 🟢 or 🔴 emoji.
Line 2: The single biggest reason today moved that direction (cite a specific number from their log).
Line 3: What tomorrow specifically needs to break even.
- Never use generic motivation. Reference real numbers only.
- Be direct, not rude. Caring boss tone, not nagging app.
- One Islamic touch is allowed if natural (e.g., "Bismillah, tomorrow:")
You are the NAFS Tribunal. You appear once per week to deliver an honest
verdict on the user's last 7 days. You speak like a wise mentor who has
already read every log — because you have.
1. THE VERDICT — one paragraph, blunt. Reference at least 3 specific numbers.
2. WHAT YOU SAID YOU WANTED — quote the user's dream statement.
3. WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID — the gap, in numbers.
4. THE ROOT CAUSE — find one underlying pattern (sleep, screen time, day-of-week, etc).
5. NEXT WEEK'S 3 RULES — three concrete, non-negotiable rules for the next 7 days.
6. ONE QURANIC OR PROPHETIC LINE — relevant to this week's pattern. Keep it short.
- Never crush a person who is genuinely struggling — distinguish "lazy" from "overwhelmed" using sleep + mood data.
- Always end with belief in the user.
"dream_statement": "Become an independent software engineer by Dec 2027",
You are reading a letter the user wrote to their future self N months ago.
Your job is to reply AS IF you are the bridge between past-self and present-self.
Para 1: Acknowledge what past-self hoped for. Quote one line from their letter.
Para 2: Show what current data says — without anger, without flattery. Just the facts.
Para 3: Tell present-self what past-self would say if they could see this. End with one
concrete action for the next 24 hours.
- Use the user's own words from their letter where possible.
- Never preach. Let the gap speak for itself.
- Tone: a quiet, wise older brother.
You are NAFS — the user's personal AI coach with full access to their last
90 days of life data. Answer their question using THEIR data, not general advice.
- Always cite at least one specific number from their data in your answer.
- If you spot a pattern in their data that explains their question, surface it.
- Speak like a wise mentor who respects Islamic values.
- Never give generic productivity tips. If you cannot ground an answer in their data, say so honestly.
- Keep responses under 200 words unless the user explicitly asks for depth.
Next.js 14 (App Router) + React + TypeScript — deployed as a PWA
Vercel free tier — generous limits, automatic HTTPS, instant deploys
Supabase free tier — Postgres, 500 MB, unlimited rows
Web Push API (works on iOS 16.4+ when added to Home Screen)
Zustand or React Server Components (no Redux needed)
~$10/year if you want nafs.app or similar
Total to run NAFS for yourself + 100 friends:
Logo, sign in/up with email or Google. 2-second auto-skip if logged in.
Name. The phrase 'who do you want to become in one sentence?'
Dream statement, dream date, dream image upload, 'why this dream?'
AI helps estimate total hours needed. Slider to confirm.
Drag/drop weights for what counts toward your dream.
Write a letter to who you will be in 1 year. Saved, surfaces in 30 days.
Sleep time, screen time limit, prayer reminder time, log time.
Identity Score %, Today's Pull, Trajectory chart, dream image, prayer countdown, big '+' log button
Single scrolling form: prayers (5 taps), dream-work hours, study, sleep, screen time, mood, photo, voice note, free text reflection.
Prayer heatmap (GitHub-style), Quran progress, dhikr counter, Hijri calendar.
Public dream board, Trajectory Forecast, Required-vs-Actual chart, weighted hours table.
Active challenges, sadqa stakes config, streak history, create new.
Today's Pull narrative, Sunday Tribunal archive, Ask NAFS chat, Future Self letters.
Searchable timeline of every daily log, filterable by photo / mood / week.
Edit dream, edit weights, notification times, export data, sign out.
All notifications are local or web-push. No SMS. No phone calls. They are the only proactive thing NAFS does.
Fajr in 20 minutes. The day starts here.
Today's required: 9.0 weighted hours. What's your first move?
Halfway. You've logged 0 dream hours. 6 hours left in productive day.
Time to log your day. 90 seconds. Be honest.
Your 12-day Quran streak is at risk. Log now.
Your weekly verdict is ready. Tap to face it.
A letter from your past self has arrived.
You owe Rs. 300 to charity. Tap for verified options.
Your forecast moved from March 2028 to June 2028. Open NAFS.
id, name, email, avatar_url, dream_id, created_at, timezone
id, user_id, statement, dream_date, image_url, total_hours_required, public_board_visible, created_at

id, user_id, date, prayers (jsonb), dream_work_hours, study_hours, screen_time_mins, sleep_start, sleep_end, mood (1–10), reflection_text, voice_note_url, photo_urls (text[]), weighted_hours_today, identity_score, todays_pull_days, created_at
id, user_id, title, duration_days, start_date, sadqa_amount, sadqa_currency, current_streak, longest_streak, status, created_at
id, challenge_id, date, completed (bool), broken_reason, sadqa_paid (bool), sadqa_receipt_url
id, user_id, content, written_at, target_deliver_date, delivered_at, ai_reply_text
id, user_id, type (tribunal/pull/gap/letter_reply), week_start, content_md, generated_at, model_used
id, user_id, messages (jsonb), created_at — for Ask NAFS
id, user_id, date, pages_read, surah, ayah_from, ayah_to, notes
Next.js + Supabase setup, auth (email + Google), PWA manifest, Add to Home Screen tested on iPhone, dark theme
Screens S1–S7, dreams + activity_weights tables, dream image upload, AI hours-estimator (Gemini), Future Self letter S6
Screen S9, daily_logs table, weighted-hours calculation, Today's Pull computation, photo upload to Supabase Storage
Screen S8 with Identity Score and Trajectory chart (Recharts), Deen tracker S10, prayer times via Aladhan, prayer heatmap
Gemini integration for Today's Pull narrator, Ask NAFS chat S13, web push setup, all 10 notification types, Vercel Cron for Sunday Tribunal
Challenges + sadqa flow S12, past logs S14, dark-mode polish, performance pass, deploy to Vercel, share link to first 5 friends
Use it daily for 30 days. Fix what's annoying. Add Urdu in v1.1 if it works.
12.1 For Ahmad personally (the only metric that matters in v1)
Identity Score 7-day average rises week over week for 8 consecutive weeks.
Average dream-work weighted hours/day rises from baseline to ≥ 6.
Sunday Tribunal opened within 24 hours: 100% of weeks.
At least one 21-day challenge completed in the first 60 days.
By month 3: subjective answer to 'are you closer to your dream?' is yes, with numbers to prove it.
12.2 If sharing later (don't optimize for this in v1)
Daily Log completion rate > 60% on active days
Median user reports 'feels honest' in qualitative feedback
In 6 months, can Ahmad open NAFS and see — with numbers — that he is closer to his independent dream than he was today?
If yes, the app worked. If no, the app failed — even if it has 10,000 users.
13. What to Do Right After This Document
One sentence. Be specific. Include a date.
2 minutes. aistudio.google.com → Get API key → Free.
2 minutes. supabase.com → New project → Free tier.
1 minute. vercel.com → Sign in with GitHub.
Pick a name to ship under.
Decide if it's just NAFS or something else. Buy the .app domain (~$10/year) only after week 4 — not before.
Auth + PWA shell + dark theme + add-to-home-screen working on your iPhone 11.
Don't post about it. Don't make a Twitter account for it. Build for 30 days in silence, ship it for yourself, USE IT for 30 days. Only THEN consider sharing.
“And whoever fears Allah — He will make for him a way out, and provide for him from where he does not expect.”
NAFS — built first for one person, to stop wasting his life and become who he said he wanted to be.
Document v2.0 • May 2026 • Prepared for Ahmad
